name: Dexter Gordon
abstract: Dexter Gordon (February 27, 1923 – April 25, 1990) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist, composer, and bandleader. He was among the most influential early bebop musicians.

concerts.label: Tribute to Charles Mingus: Mingus Music
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Stan Getz Quintet/ Dexter Gordon Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: I had worked with Dexter over there several times... I was asked to come back to do this CD with Dexter Gordon. The “Sophisticated Giant” CD.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Rufus Reid
about_quotes.evidence: He wanted it to be powerful under him as a unit... I still reap the benefits of having been part of that group for four solid years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rufus Reid interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, GA, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Scott Jimmy
about_quotes.evidence: No, they hadn’t come through yet. No. They hadn’t come through yet, because I was away from home when they did come through.
about_quotes.doc_title: Scott Jimmy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: getting Dexter on the Lionel Hampton Band, which was the first band that Dexter Gordon ever worked with.

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Bergonzi
about_quotes.evidence: you listen to early John Coltrane you can hear Dexter Gordon in it
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Bergonzi interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 7, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: When I look at your list of people that you’ve collaborated with... Dexter Gordon... Like with Dexter we had one rehearsal...
